content.problemStatement:
You are given two positive integers N,KN, KN,K, where K‚Č§NK \leq NK‚Č§N. Your goal is to find the number of permutations of {1,2,...,N}\{1, 2, ..., N\}{1,2,...,N} with exactly KKK inversions. As the answer may be very large, you only need to find it modulo 222.

###Input:


The first line contains one integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, two integers N,KN, KN,K.
###Output:
For each test case, print one integer: the number of permutation of NNN elements with KKK inversions, modulo 222.

###Constraints


1‚Č§T‚Č§1001 \leq T \leq 1001‚Č§T‚Č§100
0‚Č§K‚Č§N‚Č§2‚čÖ10100 \leq K \leq N \leq 2 \cdot 10^{10}0‚Č§K‚Č§N‚Č§2‚čÖ1010
###Subtasks


10 points : 1‚Č§N‚Č§2001 \leq N \leq 2001‚Č§N‚Č§200
10 points : 1‚Č§N‚Č§20001 \leq N \leq 20001‚Č§N‚Č§2000
10 points : 1‚Č§N‚Č§200‚ÄČ0001 \leq N \leq 200\,0001‚Č§N‚Č§200000
10 points : 1‚Č§N‚Č§2‚ÄČ000‚ÄČ0001 \leq N \leq 2\,000\,0001‚Č§N‚Č§2000000
60 points : 1‚Č§N‚Č§2‚čÖ10101 \leq N \leq 2 \cdot 10^{10}1‚Č§N‚Č§2‚čÖ1010
